source: CIS GOOGLE CLOUD PLATFORM FOUNDATION BENCHMARK V4.0.0
title: Ensure That Instances Are Not Configured To Use the Default Service Account With Full Access to All Cloud APIs

input_row.source: CIS GOOGLE CLOUD PLATFORM FOUNDATION BENCHMARK V4.0.0
input_row.title: Ensure That Instances Are Not Configured To Use the Default Service Account With Full Access to All Cloud APIs
input_row.description: To support principle of least privileges and prevent potential privilege escalation it is recommended that instances are not assigned to default service account Compute Engine default service account with Scope Allow full access to all Cloud APIs.
input_row.rationale: Along with ability to optionally create, manage and use user managed custom service accounts, Google Compute Engine provides default service account Compute Engine default service account for an instances to access necessary cloud services. Project Editor role is assigned to Compute Engine default service account hence, This service account has almost all capabilities over all cloud services except billing. However, when Compute Engine default service account assigned to an instance it can operate in 3 scopes. 1. Allow default access: Allows only minimum access required to run an Instance (Least Privileges) 2. Allow full access to all Cloud APIs: Allow full access to all the cloud APIs/Services (Too much access) 3. Set access for each API: Allows Instance administrator to choose only those APIs that are needed to perform specific business functionality expected by instance When an instance is configured with Compute Engine default service account with Scope Allow full access to all Cloud APIs, based on IAM roles assigned to the user(s) accessing Instance, it may allow user to perform cloud operations/API calls that user is not supposed to perform leading to successful privilege escalation. Impact: In order to change service account or scope for an instance, it needs to be stopped.
input_row.audit: From Google Cloud Console 1. Go to the VM instances page by visiting: https://console.cloud.google.com/compute/instances. 2. Click on each instance name to go to its VM instance details page. 3. Under the API and identity management, ensure that Cloud API access scopes is not set to Allow full access to all Cloud APIs. From Google Cloud CLI 1. List the instances in your project and get details on each instance: gcloud compute instances list --format=json | jq -r '. | "SA Scopes: \(.[].serviceAccounts[].scopes) Name: \(.[].name) Email: \(.[].serviceAccounts[].email)"' 2. Ensure that the service account section has an email that does not match the pattern [PROJECT_NUMBER]-compute@developer.gserviceaccount.com. Exception: VMs created by GKE should be excluded. These VMs have names that start with gke- and are labeled `goog-gke-node
input_row.remediation: From Google Cloud Console 1. Go to the VM instances page by visiting: https://console.cloud.google.com/compute/instances. 2. Click on the impacted VM instance. 3. If the instance is not stopped, click the Stop button. Wait for the instance to be stopped. 4. Next, click the Edit button. 5. Scroll down to the Service Account section. 6. Select a different service account or ensure that Allow full access to all Cloud APIs is not selected. 7. Click the Save button to save your changes and then click START. From Google Cloud CLI 1. Stop the instance: gcloud compute instances stop <INSTANCE_NAME> 2. Update the instance: gcloud compute instances set-service-account <INSTANCE_NAME> --service- account=<SERVICE_ACCOUNT> --scopes [SCOPE1, SCOPE2...]  3. Restart the instance: gcloud compute instances start <INSTANCE_NAME> Default Value: While creating an VM instance, default service account is used with scope Allow default access. References: 1. https://cloud.google.com/compute/docs/access/create-enable-service-accounts- for-instances 2. https://cloud.google.com/compute/docs/access/service-accounts Additional Information: • User IAM roles will override service account scope but configuring minimal scope ensures defense in depth • Non-default service accounts do not offer selection of access scopes like default service account. IAM roles with non-default service accounts should be used to control VM access.
input_row.impact: In order to change service account or scope for an instance, it needs to be stopped.
input_row.references: 1. https://cloud.google.com/compute/docs/access/create-enable-service-accounts- for-instances 2. https://cloud.google.com/compute/docs/access/service-accounts Additional Information: • User IAM roles will override service account scope but configuring minimal scope ensures defense in depth • Non-default service accounts do not offer selection of access scopes like default service account. IAM roles with non-default service accounts should be used to control VM access.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The control involves checking the service account assigned to VM instances and their API access scopes, both of which can be queried using GCP APIs and Google Cloud SDK (gcloud).
- The audit instructions provide gcloud commands to list instances, their service accounts, and scopes, indicating this can be automated.
- The Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) has clear methods to check these properties.
- This involves GCP service properties around access control, which are typically available via APIs and SDKs.

**PROGRAM NAME:** gcp_compute_instance_default_service_account_scope_check

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
1. Enumerate all VM instances in the project using the command: `gcloud compute instances list --format=json`.
2. For each instance, retrieve the service account email and API scopes using the `gcloud compute instances describe <INSTANCE_NAME> --format=json` command.
3. Check if the service account email matches the pattern `[PROJECT_NUMBER]-compute@developer.gserviceaccount.com`, which identifies the default service account.
4. Verify that the API access scopes do not include "cloud-platform" (which grants full access).
5. Exclude VM instances created by GKE that have names starting with `gke-` and are labeled `goog-gke-node`.
6. Pass criteria: Instances should not use the default service account OR should not have the "cloud-platform" scope.
7. Fail criteria: If any instance uses the default service account with "cloud-platform" scope, it should be marked as non-compliant.
step2_review:
---

### 1. **APPROACH VALIDATION:** AGREE

- **Manual vs Automated Classification:**  
  The classification as "Automated" is correct. All required information (VM instance service account and API scopes) is accessible via GCP APIs and the Google Cloud SDK (`gcloud`). No manual review is necessary unless there are edge cases not handled by automation (e.g., custom naming conventions or legacy resources).
- **GCP SDK/CLI Coverage:**  
  The following `gcloud` commands are valid and sufficient:
  - `gcloud compute instances list --format=json`
  - `gcloud compute instances describe <INSTANCE_NAME> --format=json`
- **Alternative Automation:**  
  This control could also be checked using the REST API (`compute.instances.list` and `compute.instances.get`), or with Infrastructure as Code (e.g., Terraform state), but the `gcloud` approach is direct and appropriate.

---

### 2. **TECHNICAL ACCURACY:** 8/10

- **Strengths:**
  - The automation steps are technically correct and feasible.
  - The use of service account email pattern matching is valid for identifying the default Compute Engine service account.
  - The check for the "cloud-platform" scope is appropriate for identifying overly broad permissions.
  - Excluding GKE nodes by name prefix and label is a good practice.
- **Weaknesses / Omissions:**
  - **Scoping Details:**  
    The description does not specify how to handle instances with multiple service accounts or multiple scopes. It should clarify that *any* presence of the default service account *and* "cloud-platform" scope is non-compliant, regardless of other accounts/scopes.
  - **API Scope Representation:**  
    The "cloud-platform" scope may appear as `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/cloud-platform` in the API response. The check should match the full URI, not just the string "cloud-platform".
  - **Program Name:**  
    The program name is descriptive but could be more precise. For example, `gcp_compute_instance_default_service_account_full_api_access_check` more clearly indicates the "full access" aspect.
  - **Edge Cases:**  
    The method for excluding GKE nodes is reasonable, but some environments may have custom node pool naming or labels. This should be noted as a potential limitation.
  - **Bulk Efficiency:**  
    For large environments, using `gcloud compute instances list --format='value(name,zone)'` and batching `describe` calls may be more efficient.

---

### 3. **IMPROVEMENTS:**

- **Clarify Scope Matching:**  
  Ensure the check matches the *full* scope URI:  
  `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/cloud-platform`
- **Explicitly Handle Multiple Service Accounts/Scopes:**  
  Document that the check must flag any instance where the default service account *and* the "cloud-platform" scope are both present, regardless of other accounts/scopes.
- **Program Name Suggestion:**  
  Use:  
  `gcp_compute_instance_default_service_account_full_api_access_check`
- **Alternative GCP API Methods:**  
  - Use the [Compute Engine API](https://cloud.google.com/compute/docs/reference/rest/v1/instances/list) for direct integration.
  - Consider using [Cloud Asset Inventory](https://cloud.google.com/asset-inventory/docs/overview) for organization-wide queries.
- **Edge Case Note:**  
  Document that GKE node exclusion may not catch all managed node pools if custom naming or labeling is used.
- **Output Formatting:**  
  Recommend outputting a CSV or JSON report listing non-compliant instances for easier remediation.

---

### 4. **CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

- The assessment is technically sound, and the automation approach is valid and effective for the control as described.

---

### 5. **FINAL RECOMMENDATION:** MODIFY

- **Keep the automation approach.**
- **Modify the technical implementation** as follows:
  - Match the full scope URI (`https://www.googleapis.com/auth/cloud-platform`).
  - Clarify handling of multiple service accounts/scopes.
  - Update the program name to `gcp_compute_instance_default_service_account_full_api_access_check`.
  - Add a note about possible edge cases with GKE node exclusion.
- **No need to re-classify** as Manual; Automated is correct.

---

**Summary Table:**

| Section               | Assessment           | Notes                                                                                  |
|-----------------------|---------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Approach Validation   | AGREE               | Fully automatable with gcloud/API                                                      |
| Technical Accuracy    | 8/10                | Minor improvements needed for scope matching and program naming                        |
| Improvements          | See above           | Scope URI, multi-account handling, program name, GKE edge cases                        |
| Confidence            | HIGH                | Approach is robust and aligns with GCP security best practices                         |
| Final Recommendation  | MODIFY              | Refine implementation details, but keep as Automated                                   |

---

step3_final_decision.decision_text:
**FINAL APPROACH:** Automated

**JUSTIFICATION:**  
Both AI opinions correctly identify that all necessary data (VM instance service account and API scopes) are fully accessible via GCP APIs and the Google Cloud SDK (`gcloud`). The audit steps provided in the control map directly to available CLI/API calls, and there is no requirement for manual inspection or context that cannot be programmatically determined. The only nuance is ensuring the check accounts for all assigned service accounts and all scopes, but this is feasible with the available data.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

---

**PROGRAM NAME:**  
gcp_compute_instance_default_service_account_full_api_access_check

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- GCP Resource/Service: Compute Engine VM Instances
- API/CLI Method: 
  - `gcloud compute instances list --format=json`
  - `gcloud compute instances describe <INSTANCE_NAME> --format=json`
- Properties to Check:
  - `serviceAccounts[].email` (should not be the default Compute Engine service account: `[PROJECT_NUMBER]-compute@developer.gserviceaccount.com`)
  - `serviceAccounts[].scopes` (should not include `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/cloud-platform`)
- Pass Criteria:
  - Each VM instance either does NOT use the default Compute Engine service account, OR, if it does, the assigned scopes do NOT include `cloud-platform` (full access to all Cloud APIs).
- Fail Criteria:
  - Any VM instance uses the default Compute Engine service account AND has the `cloud-platform` scope assigned.
  - (Optionally, exclude GKE nodes by name prefix `gke-` and label `goog-gke-node`.)

---

**DISAGREEMENTS RESOLVED:**  
There were no substantive disagreements between the AI opinions. Both agreed on automation feasibility, and the minor critique regarding multiple service accounts/scopes is addressed by ensuring the automation checks all assigned service accounts and all scopes per instance.

**SOURCE:** CIS GOOGLE CLOUD PLATFORM FOUNDATION BENCHMARK V4.0.0
